page: Microsoft Azure E96s_v5 is an ESv5-family virtual machine instance configured with 96 virtual CPUs and 672.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 7.0 GB RAM-to-vCPU ratio. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server utilizes Microsoft Hyper-V virtualization to deliver 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor features a 48 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration via VAES and SHA-NI. This instance does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, relying instead on external premium storage options.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities and high memory throughput. The server achieved top-tier performance in Passmark CPU encryption tests and strong results in multi-core CPU stress tests, Geekbench multi-core evaluations, and software compilation tasks. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only cached small block transfers reaching 5036.35 GB/sec and uncached large block reads at 322.18 GB/sec. However, single-core performance remains moderate, as evidenced by average ratings in single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench single-core tests.

The resource density of the E96s_v5 makes it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. While it demonstrates strong performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving, its LLM inference capabilities present a clear tradeoff. Prompt processing for small and medium models is strong, but text generation for large 70B models is weak, making it less suitable for heavy generative AI tasks. The instance is best suited for relational databases, in-memory caching layers, enterprise application backends, and multi-threaded data processing pipelines.
description: Microsoft Azure E96s_v5 is an ESv5-family virtual machine featuring 96 vCPUs and 672.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz. Built on x86_64 architecture with Hyper-V virtualization, it offers 48 physical cores and a 48 MB L3 cache. This instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on network-attached premium storage. Benchmarks show strong multi-core performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and top-tier cryptographic execution, though single-core speeds and large-model LLM text generation represent notable performance tradeoffs. Qualitatively, its high memory-to-vCPU ratio provides excellent resource density for memory-bound enterprise applications. It is highly suited for large-scale database operations, Redis caching, and multi-threaded data processing workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96s_v5 is an ESv5-family virtual machine featuring 96 vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8370C processor, delivering strong multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96s_v5 is an ESv5 virtual machine with 96 vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring ninety-six vCPUs and robust multi-core processing for demanding enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 672.0 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor
Strong multi-core performance and high-speed memory bandwidth
No local storage or GPU resources included, requiring external premium storage
Top-tier cryptographic execution and strong database operation benchmarks
Suited for in-memory databases, Redis caching, and multi-threaded enterprise applications
